content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 2, 1986


Peter Bagatelos
Bagatelos & Fadem
601 California St., Ste. 1801
San Francisco, CA 94108

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-86-202

Dear Mr. Bagatelos:

This is to confirm the advice I provided to you by telephone
on May 22, 1986, with regard to the filing obligations of your
client, Californians for an Open Primary (I.D. No. 840374),
under the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform
Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015).

FACTS

Californians for an Open Primary is a recipient committee
which was primarily formed in 1984 to qualify a measure for the
statewide ballot. The measure failed to qualify in 1984, but
the committee remained in existence and filed semi-annual cam-
paign disclosure reports during 1985. During 1986, the committee
began efforts to qualify the measure for the November 1986
general election ballot.

QUESTION

You have asked whether Californians for an Open Primary
must file a semi-annual campaign disclosure statement for the
period January 1, 1986 through June 30, 1986, pursuant to
Government Code Section 84200(a), a post-qualification state-
ment pursuant to Government Code Section 84200.5(f), or both.

ANSWER

I advised you that the committee should file the post-
qualification statement and should not file the semi-annual
statement due on July 31, 1986. The post-qualification
statement must be filed 35 days after the date of notification
that the measure has qualified or failed to qualify for the
ballot, or 35 days after the deadline for filing petitions,
whichever is earlier. The period covered by the statement will
begin on January 1, 1986, and end seven days prior to the
35-day filing deadline.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Peter Bagatelos                                   July 2, 1986
Page Two


     Enclosed is a 1986 Statement of Organization (Form 410)
which should be used by the committee to report any changes
which have occurred in the committee's status since filing
its original Statement in 1984.

     I hope the foregoing is helpful.  Please do not hesitate
to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional questions.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [signature]
                                        Carla J. Wardlow
                                        Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

cc:  Secretary of State
